sentence: Less than 3 percent of the world's water is fresh, and most of __17__ is trapped in polar ice or buried underground in springs too deep to reach.
explanation: 空格代替前面提到的不可數名詞 water（單數），most of it 表示「其中大部分」。which 需要關係子句結構，what 不構成完整名詞子句，them 是複數代名詞跟 water 不一致。

sentence: Even __19__ supplies are sufficient or plentiful, they are increasingly at risk due to various forms of pollution.
explanation: Even where 意為「即使在…的情況下」，where 引導的是抽象的情境子句，不必指實體地點：即使供水充足的地方，也日益受到污染威脅；how、why、what 都無法引導這種讓步子句。
note: 答對率 26%。【你可能會這樣想】where 你大概只用來指實體地點——公園、學校、城市，所以看到本句沒有具體地名就把它排除了。其實 where 可以引導抽象的「情境、狀況」：a situation where…（在…的情況下）、cases where…（在…的案例中）。本句 Even where supplies are sufficient ＝「即使在供水充足的地方（情況下）」。【怎麼判斷】句首的 Even 是關鍵——它需要一個讓步的框架，後面接的是完整子句 supplies are sufficient or plentiful，而這個子句不缺主詞也不缺受詞，所以要用引導副詞子句的 where，不是缺一角才用的 what。【前後文】前一句說淡水湖泊河流看似很多、其實只是杯水車薪；本句再退一步說即使充足的地方也有污染威脅——語氣是層層加重的，Even 正好承接。【常考搭配】Even where…、a situation／case where…、This is where…（這就是…之處）。

sentence: Fierce national competition __20__ water resources has prompted fears that water issues contain the seeds of violent conflict.
explanation: competition for 意為「對…的競逐」，for 帶出爭奪的標的：各國對水資源的激烈競逐引發衝突疑慮；with 用於競爭的對手（compete with sb），to、at 都不合。
note: 答對率 36%，鑑別度 35。【你可能會這樣想】competition 你認得，但它後面該接哪個介系詞常被忽略。關鍵在於分清楚「跟誰爭」與「爭什麼」：competition 「with」 sb ＝ 與某人競爭（對手）；competition 「for」 sth ＝ 為了爭奪某物（標的）。本句空格後面是 water resources（水資源），那是被爭奪的東西，不是對手，所以用 for。【動詞形也一樣】compete with sb for sth ＝ 與某人爭奪某物：Several companies are competing for the contract.（好幾家公司在爭這紙合約）。「遇到介系詞題，先問空格後面那個名詞在句中扮演什麼角色。」【文內線索】前一句說即使供水充足的地方也面臨污染威脅；本句說各國激烈競逐水資源、引發衝突疑慮；下一句說國際合作因此刻不容緩。三句層層推進，主題就是「水資源不夠、大家在搶」。【常考搭配】competition for／with、compete for a prize、in competition with、the demand for。

sentence: Each of the six science Olympiads __22__ its specific aims.
explanation: Each of + 複數名詞，主詞中心語是單數的 each，動詞須用單數 has；不受介系詞片語裡的複數 Olympiads 影響。have／are 用於複數主詞，is 語意不合（此處需要「擁有」的動詞而非be動詞）。

sentence: The purpose of the rally was to ask fans to respect the copyright and to stop buying __26__ CDs.
explanation: 過去分詞 pirated 作形容詞修飾 CDs，表示「被盜版的CD」（被動關係——CD是被盜版製作出來的）。pirating（現在分詞，主動語意不合，CD不會主動盜版）、pirate／piracy 是名詞或動詞原形，不能直接修飾名詞。

sentence: After signing million-dollar contracts with recording companies, singers devote most of their time __28__ their music perfect while companies are busy commercializing their products.
explanation: 【句型】devote + 時間/心力 + to + Ving，是固定搭配，表示「把時間投入在……」；make their music perfect（使音樂完美）用 making 呼應 to 之後的動名詞形式。其餘選項的動詞搭配（doing／singing／playing）都無法接續「perfect」形成合理語意。

sentence: There is, __16__, only a limited amount of water on the planet.
explanation: In fact（事實上）作插入語，用來強調、澄清前文說法，符合語境（強調地球水資源有限這個事實）。meanwhile（同時）、besides（此外）、for example（例如）都不符合這種「強調事實」的插入語氣。

sentence: Any IMO contest brings not only young mathematicians together but also their instructors; __25__, the IMO has its final aim to create opportunities for the exchange of information on math schedules and practice throughout the world.
explanation: 分號後延續前句邏輯：正因為比賽把數學家和指導老師聚在一起，「因此」IMO的終極目標就是促成資訊交流，therefore 表順接因果。whereas（然而，對比）、nevertheless（然而，轉折）、likewise（同樣地）都不符合這裡的順接因果語氣。

sentence: It is the fans that support the kingdoms of the recording companies; __29__, the industry never gives its feedback to the fans—by lowering the prices of CDs.
explanation: 前後句語意對比：「粉絲支撐了唱片公司的王國」vs「業界卻從未回饋粉絲」，however 表達這種轉折對比。indeed（確實）、hence（因此，因果）、furthermore（此外，累加）都不符合這裡「前後對比」的邏輯。

sentence: __27__ steel construction, buildings could then have a great many stories.
explanation: With 表示「藉由／有了……」，With steel construction（有了鋼構工法）符合「因為有這項技術，建築物才能蓋很多層樓」的因果邏輯。In／For／Beside 都無法表達這種「憑藉某條件」的語意。

sentence: Therefore, to make tall buildings more accessible to their users, the elevator __29__ .
explanation: electric elevator 是「被發明」的對象（被動關係），且敘述historical past事件用簡單過去式被動語態 was invented；invented（主動語態，語意錯誤——電梯不會主動發明）、had invented／would have invented 都是主動語態，也不符合這裡單純敘述過去發生的事實。

sentence: The survey __22__ indicated that 45 percent of the school children read at least five books every month; __23__ 45 percent of them read less than three per month.
explanation: further 作副詞表示「進一步（指出）」，用來承接、補充調查的另一項發現；thus（因此，因果）、otherwise（否則）、for instance（例如）都不符合這裡「補充說明調查結果」的語意。

sentence: The survey __22__ indicated that 45 percent of the school children read at least five books every month; __23__ 45 percent of them read less than three per month.
explanation: another 在此指「另外的 45%」，用來承接前面剛提過的 45 percent：另有四成五的學童每月讀不到三本書；other 是形容詞須接複數名詞，others' 與 the other's 為所有格，都不合。
note: 答對率 36%。【你可能會這樣想】another、other、others、the other 這四個字你背過規則，卻很少練到「接在數字前面」的用法。another 除了「再一個」，也可以放在數詞或數量詞之前，表示「另外的（同樣數量）」：another 45 percent（另外四成五）、another two weeks（再兩個星期）、another 10 dollars（再十塊錢）。「這時 another 後面接複數名詞也沒問題」，因為數量被視為一個整體。【同句就有對照】分號前面說 45 percent of the school children read at least five books every month（四成五的學童每月至少讀五本書），分號後面接 ___ 45 percent of them read less than three per month。同樣是 45%、但是不同的一群人——這正是 another 的用法。【干擾選項】other 是形容詞，要說 other students 而不能接數字；others' 與 the other's 是所有格，後面該接名詞，句構不合。【常考搭配】another + 數字 + 名詞、another two weeks、one…, another…, still another…、the other（兩者中的另一個）。

sentence: These materials, __24__ , were undreamed of in the 18th century.
explanation: 插入語 however 表示語意上的轉折對比（這些材料在現代很普遍，然而在18世紀卻是想像不到的），符合「今昔對比」的語境；finally（最後）、in addition（此外）、as a result（因此）都不符合這種轉折對比的語氣。

sentence: __26__ its strength, steel soon became a useful building material.
explanation: Because of its strength（因為它的強度）符合因果邏輯——鋼因為強度高，所以成為實用的建材。Prior to（在……之前）、In spite of（儘管）、Not to mention（更不用說）都不符合這裡的因果語意。

sentence: Some environmental scientists suggest turning newspapers __16__ charcoal.
explanation: 【句型】turn A into B 固定搭配，表示「把A轉變成B」；for／off／upon 都不是 turn 表達「轉變」的正確搭配介系詞。

sentence: This can be done by soaking sheets of newspaper in water for two hours and then __17__ them into small pieces.
explanation: by soaking...and then tearing...，前後動名詞並列對等（both follow by），tearing 呼應前面的 soaking，維持句子結構一致。tear（原形）、tore（過去式）、torn（過去分詞）都不能接在 by 之後跟 soaking 並列。

sentence: Even the youngest children can learn to see implications __24__ the obvious.
explanation: beyond the obvious 意為「超越表面所見的（深層含意）」：連最小的孩子都能學會看出言外之意；near（靠近）、among（在…之中）、without（沒有）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 28%，鑑別度只有 10。【你可能會這樣想】beyond 你大概只用在具體的距離（beyond the mountains）。把它想成「越過那條界線」，抽象用法就通了：beyond the obvious（超越表面）、beyond doubt（無庸置疑）、beyond one's reach（能力所不及）、beyond description（難以形容）。【文內線索】下一句 To read for hidden meanings is to identify with the poet（為了讀出隱藏的意義而讀，就是與詩人心靈相通）——hidden meanings（隱藏的意義）與 beyond the obvious（表面之外）是同一件事的兩種說法。【干擾選項】among（在…之中）會變成「在明顯的事物之中看出言外之意」，語意打結；near 與 without 也接不上「深層含意」的意思。【常考搭配】beyond the obvious、beyond doubt、beyond one's reach／control、go beyond。

sentence: This is reading for deeper understanding, __25__ a thoughtful look at what lies beneath the surface.
explanation: 現在分詞 taking 引導分詞片語，補充說明「深度閱讀」這件事的具體內涵（用心觀察表面之下的東西），是對前面 reading 的同位補述。take（原形）、takes（限定動詞，句子已有主要動詞 is）、to take（不定詞語意不合）都不符合這個補述結構。

sentence: Outbreaks of avian influenza __27__ be devastating for the poultry industry and for farmers.
explanation: can be devastating 表示「可能具有毀滅性」，can 表達可能性；shall（將要，較正式且不合語境）、dare（敢）、need（需要）都不符合這裡描述禽流感可能造成之影響的語意。

sentence: Economic consequences are often most serious in developing countries __29__ poultry raising is an important source of income, and of food, for impoverished rural farmers and their families.
explanation: where 引導形容詞子句修飾地方先行詞 developing countries，表示「在那些國家（養禽業是重要收入來源）」。that 通常不直接引導完整地方子句、until（直到）、although（雖然）都不符合這裡「修飾地點」的語法功能。

sentence: __19__ suggestion made by the experts is to dip newspaper sheets in vinegar and water, and use them to clean windows, mirrors, and tiles.
explanation: Another suggestion（另一個建議）承接前文已提過的第一個建議（把報紙變成木炭），引出第二個建議，符合列點論述的邏輯。This／That 是指示前文剛提過的同一件事，不是「另一個」，Other 不能直接修飾單數名詞 suggestion。

sentence: Old papers can __20__ be used to line rubbish bins and as packing material when mailing breakable goods.
explanation: also（也）表示「舊報紙也可以被用來……」，承接前文列舉的其他用途，屬於累加式列點。soon（很快）、thus（因此）、rather（相當）都不符合這種「還可以用來做……」的累加語意。

sentence: Certainly, children must start with enjoyment __22__ their interest in poetry dies.
explanation: or 在此表示「否則」：孩子必須從樂趣出發，否則他們對詩的興趣就會消失；and（而且）、so（所以）、then（然後）都無法表達這層警告語氣。
note: 答對率 36%，鑑別度 41。【你可能會這樣想】or 你大概只當它是「或者」，沒學過它接在「祈使句或 must 句」之後時，意思是「否則、不然的話」。【國中就見過這個用法】Hurry up, or you'll be late.（快一點，否則你會遲到）——前半是要求，後半是不照做的後果。本句 children must start with enjoyment or their interest in poetry dies（孩子必須從樂趣出發，否則他們對詩的興趣就會消失）結構完全一樣，只是把祈使句換成 must。【怎麼確認】看 or 後面那半句是不是「不好的結果」。本句後半是 their interest dies（興趣消失），顯然是警告而非選項，所以是「否則」不是「或者」。填 and 會變成「必須從樂趣出發，而且興趣會消失」，自相矛盾。【同義說法】or ＝ or else ＝ otherwise，三者可以互換，只是正式程度不同。【常考搭配】祈使句 + or（否則）、or else、otherwise、祈使句 + and（就會…，正面結果）。

sentence: __26__ all bird species are thought to be susceptible to infection, domestic poultry flocks are especially vulnerable to infections that can rapidly reach epidemic proportions.
explanation: While 在此是「雖然、儘管」的讓步用法：雖然所有鳥類都可能受感染，家禽群卻特別容易爆發大規模疫情；Not、Since（因為）、Unless（除非）都不合。
note: 答對率 26%，鑑別度只有 7。【你可能會這樣想】while 你先學到的一定是「當…的時候」（While I was cooking, the phone rang.），所以看到句首的 While 就往時間去想，讀不出轉折。它還有一個同樣常考的意思：「雖然、儘管」，功能等同 although。【怎麼分辨兩義】看前後兩個子句是「同時發生」還是「互相對比」。本句前半說「所有鳥類都可能受感染」，後半說「家禽群特別容易爆發疫情」——一個是普遍情況、一個是特例，屬於對比，所以是讓步用法。再看句中的 especially（特別是），也在標示這是從普遍中拉出特例。【第三個用法一起記】while 還可以表示「而、然而」，用來並列兩個相反的事實：Some people like coffee, while others prefer tea.（有些人愛咖啡，有些人則偏好茶）。【常考搭配】While…, …（雖然）、although、whereas（而、然而）、be susceptible to（易受…影響）。

sentence: For example, an outbreak of avian influenza in the USA in 1983–1984 resulted in the destruction of more than 17 million birds __28__ nearly US$ 65 million.
explanation: 【句型】at a cost of + 金額，固定搭配，表示「花費……的代價」，說明撲殺1700萬隻禽鳥所付出的經濟代價。with a view to（為了）、in regard to（關於）、on account of（因為）都不符合這裡「付出金錢代價」的語意。

sentence: When outbreaks become widespread within a country, control can be extremely difficult. __30__, government authorities usually undertake aggressive emergency control measures as soon as an outbreak is detected.
explanation: 正因為疫情擴散後控制困難，「因此」政府通常會積極採取緊急防疫措施，Therefore 表順接因果。Instead（取而代之）、By no means（絕不）、On the contrary（相反地）都不符合這裡的因果邏輯。

source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: The man then took the worm out of the first jar and put it in the second jar __18__ contained alcohol and said to the audience, “Now watch the reaction.”
explanation: which 引導形容詞子句修飾先行詞 jar，並在子句中作主詞（那個罐子裝著酒精）。what 不能有先行詞、when／where 分別修飾時間／地點先行詞，皆不符合這裡修飾「罐子」這個物品先行詞的語法。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: The New Orleans Jazz and Heritage Festival, also called Jazz Fest, is a 10-day cultural feast __21__ thousands of musicians, cooks, and craftspeople welcome 500,000 visitors each year.
explanation: in which 引導形容詞子句修飾先行詞 feast，表示「在這個盛會中，音樂家、廚師、工匠們歡迎50萬名遊客」。such that／as many as／in order for 都不是能引導修飾 feast 的關係詞結構。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: The main events include unforgettable music on 12 stages, delicious Louisiana cuisine in two large food areas, and crafts artisans from the region and __22__ the world purchasing and selling their works.
explanation: artisans from the region and around the world並列，around the world（世界各地）呼應前面from the region（來自本地），表示工匠來源涵蓋在地與全球（此為官方確認修正：原誤植為across，正確答案為around）；against／about／across均不符合此處固定搭配。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: The evening concerts __23__ different locations during the 10-day Festival.
explanation: 【句型】be held at + 地點，固定搭配，表示「（活動）在……舉辦」，符合演唱會在不同地點舉行的語意。happen to（碰巧）、are taken in（被欺騙／被收留）、participate in（參與）都不符合這裡「舉辦於」的語意。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: Teams have been known to come from __27__ to win, but the chances of overcoming that kind of lead are slim.
explanation: 【運動慣用語】come from behind 固定用法，表示「從落後局面逆轉獲勝」，符合後文「扭轉這種領先差距的機會很渺茫」的語境。above／down／underneath 都不是這個慣用語的固定搭配。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: He displayed two jars in front of the group, one containing water and the other containing alcohol, __16__ a very healthy worm.
explanation: along with（連同）表示「和一條健康的蟲一起展示」，along with a very healthy worm 補充說明展示品還包括一條蟲。in addition 通常需搭配 to 或單獨作副詞使用、on account of（因為）、as a result of（由於）都不符合這裡「一併展示」的語意。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: The group watched closely __17__ the worm floated in the jar, dangling and swimming.
explanation: as 在此表示「當……的同時」，watched as the worm floated 表示一邊觀察一邊看著蟲漂浮游動的過程。if（如果）、yet（然而）、or（或者）都不符合這種「同時觀察」的語意。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: __28__ of us can be perfectly sure about how things will turn out in the future. But we can, and often do, think of the possibilities.
explanation: None of us can be perfectly sure（我們沒有人能完全確定）呼應後句「但」我們可以、也經常會去思考各種可能性，前後形成「無法確定／但仍會臆測」的對比。All／Some／Many 都無法帶出這種與後句 But 銜接的語意對比。

year: 93年指考(敏督利颱風應變試題)
source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: We can try to guess __29__ this or that might happen.
explanation: whether 引導名詞子句作 guess 的受詞，表示「猜測是否會發生這件事或那件事」，whether...or... 是固定的「是否」句型。since／though／because 都不能引導這種作受詞的名詞子句。

source: 93年指考敏督利颱風應變試卷
sentence: Which would you wear to a football game, a sweater or a raincoat? __26__ the raincoat if you thought there was a good chance of rain.
explanation: 這是省略句修辭，Probably 承接前一句的問句「你會穿什麼？」，省略了主詞和動詞（you would wear），直接以 Probably the raincoat 簡答「大概會穿雨衣」，符合口語化的簡答語氣。Formally（正式地）、Similarly（同樣地）、Consequently（因此）都不符合這種「簡答可能性」的語意。

sentence: __19__ by the multitude of information systems requiring complicated access, Berners-Lee fashioned a universal one that made information readily __20__ .
explanation: Frustrated by… 是分詞構句，省略了 Being，表被動的「（他）因…而感到挫折」；Having frustrated 是主動完成式，To be frustrated 表目的，Been frustrated 不能單獨引導分詞構句。
note: 答對率 33%，鑑別度 35。【你可能會這樣想】看到句首要填動詞形式，四個選項都有 frustrat-，你大概靠語感亂猜。關鍵在於：frustrate 是「使人感到挫折」的情緒動詞，人是「被弄得挫折」的一方，所以要用過去分詞。【情緒動詞這一組最常錯】interest／excite／bore／surprise／frustrate 這類動詞的原意都是「使人…」，所以形容人用過去分詞（I am interested.），形容事物用現在分詞（The book is interesting.）。本句主詞是 Berners-Lee（人），當然用 Frustrated。【為什麼可以省略 Being】完整寫法是 Being frustrated by the multitude of information systems…，分詞構句裡的 Being 通常省略，只留下過去分詞。同類的還有 (Being) Born in Taipei, she…（她生於台北）。【干擾選項】Having frustrated 是主動完成式，會變成「他讓別人挫折」；To be frustrated 表目的，變成「為了感到挫折」，語意荒謬。【常考搭配】be frustrated by／with、a frustrating experience、(Being) + p.p. 分詞構句、readily available。

sentence: __19__ by the multitude of information systems requiring complicated access, Berners-Lee fashioned a universal one that made information readily __20__ .
explanation: 【句型】make + O + C，made information readily available 表示「使資訊容易取得」，呼應前文「設計一個統一系統」的目的。consistent（一致的）、important（重要的）、unforgettable（難忘的）都不符合「讓資訊容易存取」的語境。

sentence: The 7,000 pieces—full statues, heads and carved inscriptions—are breathtaking not only for their individual beauty, but also for __21__ they represent: a road map to the origins of much of Southeast Asian culture.
explanation: what 引導名詞子句作 for 的受詞，同時兼任 represent 的受詞，意思是「它們所代表的東西（即……的路線圖）」。which 需要明確先行詞、that 不能同時當介系詞受詞又當子句內受詞、when 語意不合，皆不符合。

sentence: But the collection is not in a museum, and it is not open __22__ visitors.
explanation: 【句型】open to + 對象，固定搭配，表示「對……開放」。with／by／among 都不是 open 表達「對誰開放」的正確搭配介系詞。

sentence: It is held in unknown, guarded warehouses __23__ only a few people can see it.
explanation: where 引導形容詞子句修飾地方先行詞 warehouses，表示「在那些倉庫裡，只有少數人能看到它」。which 需要子句中扮演主詞/受詞角色，此處子句已是完整句子不缺成分，故不合；until（直到）、unless（除非）都不符合修飾地點的語法功能。

sentence: He began his career as a zoologist, __28__ mollusks and their adaptations to their environment.
explanation: 現在分詞 studying 引導分詞片語，補充說明他身為動物學家時所做的事（研究軟體動物），與主要子句主詞 He 是主動關係。study（原形）、studied（過去式，不能單獨作分詞片語）、being studied（被動語態，語意錯誤——他是研究者不是被研究對象）皆不合。

sentence: __29__ age 21, he had already published 25 professional papers on that topic.
explanation: By age 21 意為「到二十一歲時（為止）」，與後面的過去完成式 had already published 搭配，表示在那個時間點之前已完成的事；In、With、On 都不能表達「到…為止」的截止概念。
note: 答對率 31%，鑑別度 44。【你可能會這樣想】表示時間的介系詞你背過 in／on／at，但 by 的「截止」概念常被忽略。by ＋ 時間點 ＝「到…之前、到…為止」，強調的是那個時間點之前累積完成的事：by then（到那時）、by the end of this year（到今年年底為止）、Finish it by Friday.（週五前完成）。【時態是最強的線索】本句用了過去完成式 had already published 25 professional papers（已經發表了二十五篇論文）——過去完成式表示「在過去某個時間點之前就完成」，而那個時間點正是由 by age 21 標出來的。看到 had + p.p.，就往「by ＋ 時間點」想。【by 與 until 別混】by 是「到…之前完成」（動作一次完成）；until 是「持續到…為止」（狀態持續）。Finish it by Friday.（週五前交）／Wait until Friday.（等到週五）。【常考搭配】by age 21、by then、by the end of…、by the time…、until。

sentence: The cab went up on the footpath and __13__ stopped centimeters away from a shop window.
explanation: finally stopped（最終停了下來）表示計程車在驚險失控後，最終在距離櫥窗僅幾公分處停下，符合整段驚險過程後平息下來的敘事節奏（此為官方確認修正：原誤植為hardly，正確答案為finally）；hardly（幾乎不）與「有停下來」的事實矛盾，likely／mainly均不符合語境。

sentence: These innovations are readily recognizable by most Americans, have had a direct impact on our everyday lives, and may also dramatically affect our lives __18__ .
explanation: 承接前面「已經對生活產生直接影響」，這句延伸談論這些創新未來持續產生的影響，in the future（在未來）符合語境。for a moment（片刻）、at that time（在那時，指過去某時間點）、from then on（從那時起，需要明確過去時間點）都不符合這裡展望未來的語意。

sentence: __25__ very large pieces too heavy to move, virtually all surviving Angkorian statues, wood carvings and artifacts have been replaced with copies.
explanation: Except for 表示「除…之外（其餘全部）」，是排除的邏輯：除了大到搬不動的文物之外，吳哥窟現存的雕像、木雕與文物幾乎全被複製品取代；In spite of（儘管）、Owing to（由於）、In addition to（除了…還有，是累加）都不合。
note: 答對率 23%。【你可能會這樣想】最危險的干擾是 In addition to——它與 Except for 的中文都能翻成「除了」，邏輯卻相反：Except for 是「扣掉」（整體中減去例外），In addition to 是「加上」（在既有的之外再添一項）。中文的「除了」同時涵蓋兩種意思，這是我們最容易翻車的地方。【怎麼分辨】看主要子句是不是在講「全部」。本句是 virtually all surviving Angkorian statues… have been replaced with copies（現存的吳哥雕像幾乎全被複製品取代）——出現了 virtually all，代表這是一個整體，前面那格自然是要扣掉搬不動的大型文物。看到 all、everything、everyone、no one，就往「排除」想。【三個一起整理】except for（整體扣除例外）、other than（不同於、另一類）、in addition to（額外再加）。【常考搭配】except for、with the exception of、apart from、in addition to、in spite of。

sentence: If you've ever seen the Sean Connery movie Medicine Man, then the annual convention of wizards and witches will be familiar to you. __19__ , get prepared for the overwhelming attack of wizards and witches here.
explanation: 承接前句「如果你看過那部電影，這個巫師大會你會覺得很熟悉」，這句是條件對比的另一半：「如果沒看過，就做好心理準備」，If not（如果沒有）呼應前面的 If you've ever seen。If any／If ever／If only 都不符合這種「前後條件對比」的邏輯。

sentence: __12__ using any thermometer, double check that it covers the right temperature __13__ for your activity or experiment.
explanation: Before using（在使用……之前）表示「使用溫度計前，要先確認……」，符合操作步驟中「事前檢查」的邏輯順序。After（之後）、For（為了）、Without（沒有）都不符合這種「使用前先確認」的語意。

sentence: __12__ using any thermometer, double check that it covers the right temperature __13__ for your activity or experiment.
explanation: temperature range（溫度範圍）是固定搭配，表示溫度計所能偵測的溫度區間。record（紀錄）、system（系統）、unit（單位）都不符合「溫度計涵蓋範圍」的語意。

sentence: A garden thermometer, __14__ , will burst if you try to use it for boiling liquids.
explanation: for instance（例如）作插入語，舉出「花園溫度計」作為前述概念（溫度計要選對範圍）的具體例子。in short（簡言之）、in turn（依序、轉而）、by contrast（相比之下）都不符合這裡「舉例說明」的語意。

sentence: __16__ since pre-Hispanic times, Lake Catemaco has been a centre for alternative medicine and strange treatments.
explanation: 【句型】Ever since + 時間點，固定搭配，表示「自……以來一直」，符合「卡特馬科湖自前西班牙時期以來就一直是……中心」的語意。Not／Only／Rarely 都不能這樣修飾 since 構成「自從……」的意思。

sentence: __17__ , the area's association with witchcraft dates back to Olmec times more than 2,000 years ago.
explanation: In fact（事實上）作插入語，用來強調、補充說明前文的巫術傳統其實可追溯到更早的奧爾梅克時期。In advance（事先）、In all（總共）、In sum（總之，通常用於總結段落）都不符合這裡「進一步強調事實」的語氣。

sentence: If you've ever seen the Sean Connery movie Medicine Man, __18__ the annual convention of wizards and witches will be familiar to you.
explanation: If...then... 是固定的條件句型，then 承接 If 子句，引出「那麼……就會……」的結果。since（因為/自從）、so（所以，通常不與if連用）、though（雖然）都不符合這種標準條件句結構。

sentence: Studies of museum behavior show that the average visitor spends about four seconds looking at one object. For young visitors, the time span can be __22__ shorter.
explanation: even + 比較級（even shorter）表示「甚至更短」，強調程度上更進一步，符合「一般遊客已經很短了，年輕遊客更短」的語意遞進。almost（幾乎，不修飾比較級表遞進）、also（也）、meanwhile（同時）都不符合這種強調比較級的用法。

sentence: It is reported that young visitors get more out of a visit if they focus on __25__ nine objects.
explanation: 【句型】no more than + 數字，表示「不超過……」，符合「聚焦在不超過九件展品上，能讓年輕遊客收穫更多」的語意。no better than（不比……好，語意不合）、no less than（不少於，語意相反）、no sooner than（一……就……，用於時間句型）都不符合。

sentence: There are about fifty animal figures __27__ 275 meters long, including a monkey, a spider, and an “owl man.”
explanation: 【句型】up to + 數字，表示「長達／最多到……」，up to 275 meters long 符合描述動物圖案尺寸範圍上限的語意。far from（遠非）語意不合、such as（例如）用於舉例而非數字範圍、as much as 通常修飾不可數量詞而非長度單位，均不合。

sentence: Some people believe the Nazca Indians were __29__ able to fly, perhaps in balloons.
explanation: somehow 意為「以某種（不明的）方式」：有人相信納斯卡人不知用什麼方法能夠飛行，或許是搭熱氣球；someday（將來某天）、sometime（某個時候）、somewhere（某處）都與句意不合。
note: 答對率 34%，鑑別度 38。【你可能會這樣想】四個 some- 開頭的字長得極像，你大概靠語感挑一個。它們的差別其實很整齊：somehow ＝ 以某種方式（不知道怎麼做到的）；someday ＝ 將來某一天；sometime ＝ 某個時候；somewhere ＝ 某個地方。「看空格前後缺的是「方式」「時間」還是「地點」。」【本句缺的是方式】the Nazca Indians were ___ able to fly（納斯卡人…能夠飛行）——句子後面接 perhaps in balloons（或許是搭熱氣球），這個 perhaps 正好呼應 somehow 的不確定語氣：怎麼飛的沒人知道。【前一句是前提】More mysterious is the fact that they can be viewed only from the air.（更神祕的是這些圖案只能從空中看見）——正因為只能從空中看，才有人猜他們會飛。【常考搭配】somehow（不知怎麼地）、someday、sometime、somewhere、somewhat（有點）。

sentence: In a British study between 1991 and 2003, people were asked how satisfied they are __11__ their lives.
explanation: 【句型】satisfied with 固定搭配，表示「對……感到滿意」。for／at／of 都不是 satisfied 正確的搭配介系詞。
fullPassage: Recent studies show that levels of happiness for most people change throughout their lives. In a British study between 1991 and 2003, people were asked how satisfied they are __11__ their lives. The resulting statistics graph shows a smile-shaped curve. Most of the people __12__ happy and become progressively less happy as they grow older. For many of them, the most miserable period in their life is their 40s. __13__, their levels of happiness climb. Furthermore, it seems that men are slightly happier on average than women in their teens, but women bounce back and overtake men __14__ in life. The low point seems to last longer for women—throughout their 30s and 40s, only climbing __15__ women reach 50. Men, on the other hand, have the lowest point in their 40s, going up again when they reach 50.

sentence: For many of them, the most miserable period in their life is their 40s. __13__ , their levels of happiness climb.
explanation: After that（在那之後）承接前句提到的「40多歲是最痛苦的時期」，表示過了這段時期後快樂程度回升。By that time（到那時為止）、Not for long（不會太久）、Before now（在此之前）都不符合這種「之後」的時間順接語意。
fullPassage: Recent studies show that levels of happiness for most people change throughout their lives. In a British study between 1991 and 2003, people were asked how satisfied they are __11__ their lives. The resulting statistics graph shows a smile-shaped curve. Most of the people __12__ happy and become progressively less happy as they grow older. For many of them, the most miserable period in their life is their 40s. __13__, their levels of happiness climb. Furthermore, it seems that men are slightly happier on average than women in their teens, but women bounce back and overtake men __14__ in life. The low point seems to last longer for women—throughout their 30s and 40s, only climbing __15__ women reach 50. Men, on the other hand, have the lowest point in their 40s, going up again when they reach 50.

sentence: Furthermore, it seems that men are slightly happier on average than women in their teens, but women bounce back and overtake men __14__ in life.
explanation: later in life 意為「在人生較晚的階段」：女性在青少年時期不如男性快樂，但後來會回升並超越男性；sooner、earlier、slower 都與後文所述的時間順序不符。
fullPassage: Recent studies show that levels of happiness for most people change throughout their lives. In a British study between 1991 and 2003, people were asked how satisfied they are __11__ their lives. The resulting statistics graph shows a smile-shaped curve. Most of the people __12__ happy and become progressively less happy as they grow older. For many of them, the most miserable period in their life is their 40s. __13__, their levels of happiness climb. Furthermore, it seems that men are slightly happier on average than women in their teens, but women bounce back and overtake men __14__ in life. The low point seems to last longer for women—throughout their 30s and 40s, only climbing __15__ women reach 50. Men, on the other hand, have the lowest point in their 40s, going up again when they reach 50.
note: 答對率 32%，鑑別度 36。【你可能會這樣想】later 你認得，難在要從前後文抓出「時間軸」的方向。本句前半說 men are slightly happier… than women in their teens（青少年時期男性略為快樂），後半用 but 轉折說女性 bounce back and overtake men ___ in life——既然前半講的是青少年時期，後半的時間點自然要往後推，所以是 later in life（人生後半段）。【下一句再次確認】The low point seems to last longer for women—throughout their 30s and 40s（女性的低潮持續到三、四十歲）——低潮拖得久，超越男性當然是更晚的事。【in life 這個說法】later in life（人生較晚的階段）、early in life（人生早期）、all one's life（一輩子）——這裡的 life 指的是「一生」這條時間軸，不是「生活」。【常考搭配】later in life、early in life、bounce back（回升、東山再起）、overtake（超越）。

sentence: The low point seems to last longer for women—throughout their 30s and 40s, only climbing __15__ women reach 50.
explanation: once 在此是從屬連接詞「一旦…就」，引導時間副詞子句：女性的低潮要一直持續到五十歲才開始回升；unless（除非）語意相反，before（在…之前）時間順序顛倒，since（自從、因為）也不合。
fullPassage: Recent studies show that levels of happiness for most people change throughout their lives. In a British study between 1991 and 2003, people were asked how satisfied they are __11__ their lives. The resulting statistics graph shows a smile-shaped curve. Most of the people __12__ happy and become progressively less happy as they grow older. For many of them, the most miserable period in their life is their 40s. __13__, their levels of happiness climb. Furthermore, it seems that men are slightly happier on average than women in their teens, but women bounce back and overtake men __14__ in life. The low point seems to last longer for women—throughout their 30s and 40s, only climbing __15__ women reach 50. Men, on the other hand, have the lowest point in their 40s, going up again when they reach 50.
note: 答對率僅 12%，是全站最難的一題。【你可能會這樣想】once 在你腦中大概只有兩個身分：副詞「曾經」（I once lived in Japan.）與「一次」（once a week）。很少人學過它也可以當從屬連接詞，意思是「一旦…就」，後面接完整子句。看到句子需要連接詞時，反而完全聯想不到這個字——這正是本題答對率只有 12% 的原因。【文法依據】once 當連接詞時等於 as soon as，用來標示「某件事一發生，另一件事就跟著發生」：Once you understand the rule, the rest is easy.（一旦懂了規則，其餘就簡單了）。本句 only climbing once women reach 50 就是「一直要到女性年滿五十，快樂程度才開始回升」。【文內驗證：下一句用了同義的說法】答案其實寫在下一句：Men, on the other hand, have the lowest point in their 40s, going up again when they reach 50.（男性則是四十幾歲最低落，到五十歲才又回升）——作者描述男性時用 when they reach 50，描述女性時用 once women reach 50，同一個意思換了一個連接詞。前後對照，答案立刻現形。【干擾選項】unless 是「除非」，會變成「除非女性年滿五十，否則才回升」，語意打結；before 是「在…之前」，時間順序剛好顛倒——低潮是持續「到」五十歲，不是五十歲「之前」才結束；since 當連接詞是「自從」或「因為」，兩義放進來都不通。【常考搭配】once + S + V（一旦…就）、as soon as、the moment（that）、once in a while（偶爾）、at once（立刻／同時）。

sentence: Science is still not certain __16__ exactly what these lights are and what causes them.
explanation: 【句型】certain as to + 問題，as to 表示「關於」，not certain as to exactly what...是固定用法，表示「對於……究竟是什麼並不確定」。due to（由於）、instead of（而不是）、in spite of（儘管）都不符合這裡的搭配。
fullPassage: The northern lights, known as the aurora borealis, is one of nature's most dazzling spectacles. Science is still not certain __16__ exactly what these lights are and what causes them. Sometimes, the brilliant rays of light spread upward in the shape of a fan. __17__, they flash here and there like giant searchlights. Farther north, the aurora frequently looks like fiery draperies which hang from the sky and sway __18__ while flames of red, orange, green, and blue play up and down the moving folds. According to scientific measurements, this discharge of light __19__ from 50 to 100 miles above the earth. It is seen __20__ around the Hudson Bay region in Canada, in northern Scotland, and in southern Norway and Sweden.

sentence: Sometimes, the brilliant rays of light spread upward in the shape of a fan. __17__ , they flash here and there like giant searchlights.
explanation: At other times 意為「在其他時候」，與前一句句首的 Sometimes 呼應：極光有時像扇形向上散開，有時則像巨大探照燈四處掃射；At one time（曾經）、At all times（隨時）、At the same time（同時）都無法構成這組呼應。

note: 答對率 35%，鑑別度 34。【你可能會這樣想】fill 你認得「填滿」，但 fill out 這個片語動詞若沒特別記過，在四個都以 out／in／off／on 結尾的選項裡就分不出來。fill out ＝ 填寫表格，是生活中極常用的說法：Please fill out this form.（請填寫這張表格）。英式英文常說 fill in，兩者意思相同。【線索是主詞】主詞是 4,017 questionnaires（問卷），問卷能被「怎麼樣」？當然是被填寫。副詞 properly（確實地、正確地）也在配合——properly filled out 是「填寫完整無誤」的固定說法。【干擾選項】take in 是「吸收、理解」，也有「欺騙」的意思（be taken in by a scam 被騙）；give off 是「散發（氣味、光）」；pick on 是「找某人碴、欺負」。【常考搭配】fill out／fill in a form、hand in（繳交）、take in、give off、pick on sb。

note: 答對率 33%。【你可能會這樣想】squeeze 你大概只記得「擠檸檬、擠牙膏」，加上 out 之後不確定意思有沒有變。其實就是字面的延伸：squeeze out ＝ 把裡面的東西擠出來，最常搭配的就是水分或液體：Squeeze out the excess water before hanging the towel.（把多餘的水擠掉再晾毛巾）。【線索是整段的製作流程】前一句說紙張被壓成球狀；本句說水分被擠出後拿去曬乾，才能當柴火替代品。浸泡 → 壓成球 → 擠水 → 曬乾，順序清清楚楚，中間這一步只能是「擠出水分」。【干擾選項】boiled down 是「熬煮濃縮」，需要加熱，但下一步才要拿去曬太陽，不合流程；fired up 是「點燃、激勵」；kicked off 是「開始」。【常考搭配】squeeze out、squeeze into（擠進）、boil down to（歸結為）、kick off（開始）。

sentence: These do not occur always as __21__ steps but may happen simultaneously.

sentence: In creating the list, the group __17__ twenty-five non-medically related technological innovations that have become widely used since 1980.

sentence: These people assembled it by removing figures from the ruins of Cambodia's world-famous temples and replacing them with hollow, concrete __24__.

note: 答對率 33%，鑑別度 48。【你可能會這樣想】fake 你大概只認得形容詞「假的」（a fake watch），很少見到它直接當名詞用，意思是「贗品、仿製品」：The painting turned out to be a fake.（那幅畫結果是贗品）。【形容詞先幫你篩掉三個選項】空格前面是 hollow, concrete（中空的、混凝土的）——能被「中空、混凝土」修飾的只能是實體的東西。strategies（策略）、courses（課程）、ways（方法）都是抽象概念，混凝土做不出來。看到形容詞很具體時，先問空格能不能被它形容，往往一步就篩掉大半。【下一句確認】For the most part, these are hard to distinguish from the originals.（這些大多難以與真品區分）——既然要與 originals（真品）對比，空格自然是「仿品」。【常考搭配】a fake（贗品）、fake news、be hard to distinguish from、the original（真品、原作）。

sentence: At age 10, he managed his first __26__, a description of a partly albino sparrow he observed in a public park.

note: 答對率 13%，鑑別度只有 4，代表高分群與低分群一起猜。【你可能會這樣想】最大的干擾來自 tended——tend to V（傾向於）是課本操練最多的句型，眼熟到你看到 to V 就直覺套上去，沒有回頭看語意合不合。但「傾向於建立一套心理學體系」語氣太弱，與後半句 became a very influential figure in educational psychology（成為教育心理學極具影響力的人物）搭不起來。【字義依據】seek 的意思是「尋求、追求」，接不定詞時 seek to V 表示「設法、致力於」，語氣積極而持續。本句 Piaget sought to establish a body of psychology all his life 就是「皮亞傑終其一生致力於建立一套心理學體系」——all his life（終其一生）正是這個語氣的佐證。【拼字：把 -ought 這一組一起記】seek 的三態是 seek-sought-sought，與 buy-bought-bought、bring-brought-brought、think-thought-thought、fight-fought-fought 同屬 -ought 家族。你如果不熟這個變化，看到 sought 可能根本認不出是 seek。【干擾選項】forget to V 是「忘記要做某事」；afford 要說 afford to V（負擔得起），且通常與金錢或時間搭配；tended 如前所述語氣太弱。【常考搭配】seek to V（設法）、seek help／advice／shelter（尋求協助／建議／庇護）、much sought-after（炙手可熱的）、hide-and-seek（捉迷藏）、tend to V（傾向於）。

sentence: Most scientists now use the International System of measures, with meters for length, kilograms for __15__, and seconds for time.

sentence: Most witch doctors are guaranteed to be charlatans, not real doctors, who __20__ some quick money from tourists.

note: 答對率 18%，鑑別度 25。【你可能會這樣想】go after 你大概只熟它字面的具體動作——「追趕」（The dog went after the cat.），沒學過它可以用在抽象的目標上，表示「追求、圖謀」：go after a job（謀職）、go after the championship（力求奪冠）、go after money（貪圖金錢）。本句 who go after some quick money from tourists 就是「圖的是從觀光客身上快速撈一筆」。【文內驗證】空格所在的關係子句修飾的是 charlatans（江湖術士、騙子）——句子已經先說 Most witch doctors are guaranteed to be charlatans, not real doctors（多數巫醫肯定是騙子，不是真正的醫生），既然是騙子，後面接的動作自然是「撈錢」。charlatans 這個字就是判斷語意方向的關鍵。【與學測題庫對照】學測 110 年那篇有一句 I don't believe God is after me（我不相信上帝在找我麻煩），用的是 be after sb（追捕、找某人麻煩）——go after 與 be after 同源，核心都是「朝某個目標追過去」。兩題可以一起記。【干擾選項】turn down 是「拒絕」，騙子不會拒絕錢；give away 是「贈送、洩漏」，方向相反；complain about 是「抱怨」，語意不合。【常考搭配】go after money／a job／a goal（追求）、be after sb（追捕）、go for it（放手一搏）、quick money（快錢）、charlatan（江湖術士）。

explanation: amazing（令人驚奇的）符合「這些線條只能從空中被看見」這件事令人驚訝的語意；hesitating／interacting／satisfying均不符合此處語境。

sentence: The only thing certain is that the Nazca lines remain one of the world's most fascinating __30__ mysteries.
explanation: unsolved mysteries（未解之謎）為固定搭配，表示納斯卡線至今仍是世界上最引人入勝的未解謎團；unchanged／undetermined／unfolded均不符合mysteries的固定搭配語意。

sentence: Most of the people __12__ happy and become progressively less happy as they grow older.
explanation: start off 意為「一開始（處於某種狀態）」：多數人一開始是快樂的，隨著年紀增長逐漸不快樂；end up（最後落得）方向相反，pass by（經過）、go on（繼續）語意也不合。
note: 答對率 35%。【你可能會這樣想】start off 與 end up 是一組相反的說法，你大概兩個都眼熟，一時分不清該用哪個。start off ＝ 一開始是…；end up ＝ 最後變成…。看句子講的是「起點」還是「終點」。【本句講的是起點】Most of the people ___ happy and become progressively less happy as they grow older.——後半句已經說「隨著年紀增長逐漸不快樂」，那是後來的變化；空格所在的前半句自然是在講「一開始」。and 前後是一個時間順序：先快樂 → 後來逐漸不快樂。【整段的圖像】前一句說統計圖呈現「微笑形狀的曲線」（a smile-shaped curve）——兩端高、中間低。高的左端就是年輕時的快樂，這正是 start off happy。下一句說最低點落在四十幾歲，也吻合。【常考搭配】start off／start out（一開始）、end up（最後落得）、as they grow older、progressively（逐漸地）。

sentence: Scientists expect it to increase an __21__ 2° to 6° F over the next one hundred years.
explanation: additional 2° to 6° F（額外的2到6華氏度）表示在過去一世紀已上升近1°F的基礎上，未來一百年還會再增加2到6度；extreme／immediate／original均不符合此處語意。

sentence: How people and nature __24__ climate change will determine how seriously it affects human health.
explanation: adapt to climate change（適應氣候變遷）表示人類與自然如何適應氣候變遷，將決定其對人類健康的影響程度；result from（起因於）、count on（依賴）、stand for（代表）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Many experts in the field believe that future phones will not only look very different---they may not even be __11__.
explanation: seen（被看見）呼應下文They may be hidden in jewelry or accessories, or even embedded in the body（可能藏在珠寶配件中，甚至被植入體內），表示未來手機可能連被看見都很難；heard／sold／changed均不符合此處語境。

sentence: They will undoubtedly have a lot of additional features and __12__ functions, and users may interact with them in new ways, too.
explanation: novel 在此是形容詞「新穎的」，novel functions 指手機將具備的新奇功能；remote（遙遠的）、scarce（稀少的）、accidental（意外的）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 26%。【你可能會這樣想】novel 你大概只記得名詞「小說」，完全沒想到它也是形容詞，意思是「新穎的、新奇的」：a novel idea（新穎的點子）、a novel approach（新的做法）。名詞義與形容詞義乍看無關，但源頭都是「新」——小說之所以叫 novel，正是因為它當年是一種「新的」文體。【同句就有線索】空格所在句的結尾是 users may interact with them in new ways, too（使用者也會以新的方式與手機互動）——句尾的 new 與 too 直接把答案講出來了：additional features 與 ___ functions 講的都是「新」。【干擾選項】remote 是「遙遠的」，remote control（遙控器）的印象太強，很多人一看到就選；但「遙遠的功能」不成話。scarce（稀少的）與 accidental（意外的）語意也不合。【常考搭配】a novel idea／approach／function、novelty（新奇）、remote control、be scarce。

sentence: Research shows that the substances __17__ these colors actually protect plants from chemical damage.
explanation: be responsible for 在此是「造成、導致」而非「負責」：造成這些顏色的物質其實能保護植物免於化學傷害；capable of（有能力）、different from（不同於）、inferior to（劣於）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 19%。【你可能會這樣想】be responsible for 你只學過「對…負責」（You are responsible for your own safety.），主詞通常是人。用在事物上時意思會轉成「造成、導致」——主詞是原因、for 後面是結果：Smoking is responsible for many deaths.（吸菸造成許多死亡）。本句 the substances responsible for these colors ＝ 造成這些顏色的物質。【線索在下一句】The colors come mainly from chemicals known as antioxidants.（這些顏色主要來自抗氧化劑）——come from 與 be responsible for 講的是同一件事，一個從結果往回看、一個從原因往前看。【同義說法】account for、be to blame for（專用於壞事：Who is to blame for the accident?）、lead to、result in、bring about。【常考搭配】be responsible for、be to blame for、account for、result in、be capable of + V-ing。

sentence: Research on how chemicals in blueberries affect brain function even suggests that these chemicals may help our own brains work more __20__.
explanation: work more efficiently（運作更有效率）表示這些化學物質能幫助大腦運作得更有效率，呼應全文「吃得健康也能讓人更聰明」的主題；obviously／diligently／superficially均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Since its way of altering moods is generally expected and socially acceptable, oftentimes it __22__ over drinking.
explanation: result in（導致）表示因為酒精帶來的心理改變被社會普遍接受，往往因此演變成／導致過量飲酒的結果；applies to／arrives at／plans on均不符合此處因果語意。

sentence: Another reason why alcohol is the main gateway drug is that the __25__ of teenagers it can affect is very wide.
explanation: population 在此指「（青少年）族群、人口」：酒精能影響的青少年族群範圍非常廣；popularity（受歡迎程度）、pollution（污染）、possibility（可能性）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 33%。【你可能會這樣想】這題是典型的形似字陷阱——population 與 popularity 只差幾個字母，趕時間時很容易看錯。population ＝ 人口、族群；popularity ＝ 受歡迎的程度（gain popularity 大受歡迎）。【線索在句尾與下一句】句尾 is very wide（範圍非常廣）——「廣」形容的是族群範圍，不是受歡迎程度；下一句 It is easily accessible in most societies and common in popular events such as sports gatherings（酒精在多數社會都容易取得，在運動聚會等場合也很常見）進一步說明「能觸及的人很多」。【順帶記一個用法】population 不只指一國人口，也可以指某個特定群體：the elderly population（高齡族群）、the student population（學生群體）。【常考搭配】the population of、gain popularity、be accessible to、a gateway drug（入門毒品）。

sentence: For one hour on that day, a huge paper-cutting machine was set up in Times Square so people could __27__ their lingering bad memories.
explanation: destroy（消除、忘卻）在此指透過碎紙機銷毀、忘卻揮之不去的壞回憶，符合時代廣場該活動（Good Riddance Day）的本意；maintain（維持）、dislike（不喜歡）、create（創造）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Recycling cans were also provided for items such as __29__ CDs and regrettable fashion mistakes.
explanation: annoying 意為「令人厭煩的」：回收桶也開放丟棄惱人的 CD 與後悔的流行打扮；available（可取得的）、amusing（有趣的）、artificial（人造的）都與「丟進回收桶」的語境不合。
note: 答對率 33%，鑑別度 46。【你可能會這樣想】四個選項都是形容詞，文法上都填得進去，只能靠語境判斷——這是本題的難處。【抓住「丟掉」這個情境】整篇在講一場「銷毀壞回憶」的活動：前男女友的照片、慘不忍睹的成績單都可以拿去碎掉。本句說回收桶也開放丟 ___ CDs 與 regrettable fashion mistakes（讓人後悔的流行打扮）。既然是拿去丟的東西，形容詞必然是負面的；而且 annoying 與並列的 regrettable 語氣一致。「看到 and 並列兩個成分時，先看已知的那一個是什麼語氣，另一個通常同向。」【干擾選項】amusing 是「有趣的」，有趣的 CD 不會拿去丟；available（可取得的）與 artificial（人造的）語意都不相干。【常考搭配】annoying／irritating（惱人的）、be annoyed at、regrettable、recycling can（回收桶）。

sentence: Students in the university's School of __11__ work with the "patient" to gain experience in effective communication and decision-making.
explanation: School of Pharmacy（藥學院）呼應全文主題train the pharmacists of the future（訓練未來的藥師），表示這些學生是藥學院的學生；Education／Business／Humanities均不符合此處語境。

sentence: The "patient" responds verbally or with gestures to indicate __12__ such as pain, stress or anxiety.
explanation: emotions such as pain, stress or anxiety（諸如疼痛、壓力或焦慮等情緒）表示虛擬病人會表達出這些情緒反應；expressions／elements／events均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...the 'patient' can be programmed to be allergic to certain medicine and can __14__ serious reactions if student learners are not aware of the situation.
explanation: suffer from 意為「出現、遭受（不良反應）」：虛擬病人可被設定成對某種藥物過敏，若學習者未察覺就會出現嚴重反應；adapt to（適應）、break into（闖入）、provide with（提供）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 34%。【你可能會這樣想】suffer 你大概只記得「受苦」，而且直覺以為主詞一定是人。其實 suffer from 的主詞可以是任何承受負面狀況的一方，本句主詞是那個虛擬病人（the "patient"）：can suffer serious reactions ＝ 會出現嚴重反應。【一個常錯的細節】suffer from 用於疾病或長期困擾（suffer from a headache／depression）；若後面接的是一次性的損失或後果，from 可以省略：suffer serious injuries／heavy losses（遭受重傷／重大損失）。【干擾選項】provide with 是「提供」，但句型是 provide sb with sth，主詞該是提供者，本句主詞是承受的一方，方向顛倒；adapt to 是「適應」，break into 是「闖入」。【常考搭配】suffer from a disease、suffer losses／injuries、be allergic to（對…過敏）、provide sb with sth。

sentence: This kind of practice allows students to learn from mistakes in a safe environment that would not be __15__ with textbooks alone.
explanation: would not be possible with textbooks alone 意為「單靠課本做不到」：這種在安全環境中從錯誤中學習的練習，光靠課本無法做到；exciting、necessary、important 都無法與 would not be…with textbooks alone 構成合理語意。
note: 答對率 28%。【你可能會這樣想】這題的難處不在單字，在句子的結構：a safe environment that would not be ___ with textbooks alone，要把 would not be、with textbooks alone 三個部分合起來讀，才知道整句在說「單靠課本辦不到」。【怎麼判斷】前一句舉例說明模擬病人可以設定成對某種藥物過敏、學生用錯藥就會出現嚴重反應。這種「犯了錯也不會真的害到人」的環境，正是課本給不了的。所以空格要填 possible——與 would not be 合起來是「不可能存在」。【句中的 alone 也是線索】alone 放在名詞後面時是「光靠、僅僅」（Money alone can't buy happiness.），本句 with textbooks alone ＝「單靠課本」，語氣本身就在暗示「還需要別的東西」。【常考搭配】would not be possible、make sth possible、if possible、with…alone（單靠）、learn from mistakes。

sentence: By the beginning of the 21st century, however, the custom had deteriorated to a point whereby the groom and his family had become very __18__.
explanation: greedy（貪婪的）呼應後文demands for dowry are not met（嫁妝要求未被滿足）及bride is subject to torture（新娘遭受虐待），表示新郎與其家人變得貪得無厭；pleasant／regretful／sympathetic均不符合此處語境。

sentence: When demands for dowry are not met, the bride is __19__ torture, and often even killed.
explanation: be subject to 意為「遭受、易受」：當嫁妝的要求未被滿足時，新娘會遭受虐待，甚至常被殺害；aware of（察覺）、required by（被…要求）、furious with（對…生氣）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 22%。【你可能會這樣想】subject 你最熟的是名詞「科目」與「主題」。它當形容詞、後面接 to 時意思是「遭受、易受」，be subject to 必須整組記。本句 the bride is subject to torture ＝ 新娘會遭受虐待。【兩層常見用法】一是「遭受、易受」：These products are subject to damage in transit.（這些產品在運送中容易受損）；二是「須經…、以…為條件」：The plan is subject to approval.（該計畫須經核准）／Prices are subject to change without notice.（價格如有變動恕不另行通知）。共同核心都是「受制於某個外力」。【文內線索】前一句說這個習俗惡化到新郎與其家人予取予求；本句 When demands for dowry are not met（當嫁妝要求未被滿足時）是條件，後面接的必然是新娘承受的後果——and often even killed 更把嚴重性推到極致。【常考搭配】be subject to torture／change／approval、subject to change without notice、be aware of、be furious with。

sentence: In fact, the Earth __11__ 20,000 times more energy from the sun than we currently use.
explanation: receives energy from the sun（從太陽接收能量）表示地球接收到的太陽能是目前人類使用量的兩萬倍；repeats／rejects／reduces均不符合此處語意。

sentence: In contrast, fossil fuels, such as oil or coal, release __14__ substances into the air when they are burned.
explanation: harmful substances（有害物質）表示化石燃料燃燒時會釋放有害物質到空氣中，與前文太陽能是「乾淨能源」形成對比；diligent／usable／changeable均不符合此處語意。

sentence: But one special sculpture gallery invites art lovers to allow their hands to __16__ the works.
explanation: run over 在此意為「（手）掠過、撫過」：這座特別的雕塑展區邀請愛好者用手撫過作品；fix up（修理、安排）、take away（拿走）、knock off（打掉、下班）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 37%。【你可能會這樣想】run over 你大概只想到「輾過」（The dog was run over by a car.），所以覺得放在這裡很奇怪。其實 run 的核心是「移動、掠過」，主詞換成手或眼睛時，意思就是「輕輕掠過、快速看過」：She ran her fingers over the keyboard.（她的手指掠過鍵盤）／Let me run over the main points again.（讓我再快速複習一次重點）。【線索是整段的對比】前一句說羅浮宮到處都是「請勿觸摸」的告示；本句用 But 轉折，說有一個展區反而邀請大家動手。下一句說明那是 the Tactile Gallery（觸覺展區），為視障者而設，是館內唯一可以觸摸的空間。既然主題是「用手觸摸」，動作自然是手在作品上撫過。【常考搭配】run one's fingers over、run over the main points（快速複習）、be run over by a car、keep one's hands off（別碰）。

sentence: Its latest exhibit is a __18__ of sculpted lions, snakes, horses and eagles.
explanation: a collection of sculpted animals（一系列雕塑動物的收藏）表示最新展覽是一批獅子、蛇、馬和鷹的雕塑收藏；cooperation／completion／contribution均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Called "Animals, Symbols of Power," the exhibit __19__ animals that were used by kings and emperors throughout history to symbolize the greatness of their reigns.
explanation: feature 在此是動詞「以…為主角、展出」：這場名為「動物，權力的象徵」的展覽，展出歷代帝王用來象徵治世昌隆的動物；examines（檢視）、protects（保護）、represents（代表）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 39%，鑑別度 42。【你可能會這樣想】feature 你大概只認得名詞「特色、特徵」（a special feature），沒學過它當動詞的用法：以…為主要內容、由…擔綱演出。The film features a young Japanese actress.（這部片由一位年輕日本女星主演）／The exhibit features 15 animals.（這場展覽以十五種動物為主角）。【線索是主詞】主詞是 the exhibit（展覽），展覽能對動物做什麼？只能是「展出、以之為主題」。前一句 The 15 animals exhibited are reproductions of famous works（展出的十五種動物是名作的複製品）已經用了 exhibited，本句只是換個動詞再講一次。【同一個字兩種詞性一起記】feature（名詞：特色）／feature（動詞：以…為主角）／be featured in（被報導）——本題庫 108 年那題考的正是第三種。【常考搭配】feature（以…為主角）、a special feature、be featured in the media、an exhibit（展覽）。

sentence: However, some requests for adjustment must be __12__, and an adjustment refusal message must be sent.
explanation: denied（被拒絕）呼應後文an adjustment refusal message（拒絕調整的訊息），表示某些調整要求必須被拒絕；retailed／appreciated／elaborated均不符合此處語意。

sentence: They are necessary when the customer is __13__ or when the vendor has done all that can reasonably or legally be expected.
explanation: at fault（有過錯的／出差錯的）表示當顧客本身理虧或有疏失，或是廠商已盡合理合法責任時，就應發出拒絕調整的訊息；on call／in tears／off guard均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Its traditional __27__ is based on forestry, animal husbandry and subsistence agriculture.
explanation: traditional economy（傳統經濟）呼應後文these account for less than 50% of the GDP now（如今這些僅佔國內生產毛額(GDP)不到一半），表示不丹過往的傳統經濟型態以林業、畜牧業與自給農業為基礎；culture／politics／religion均不符合後文GDP的總體經濟學語境。

sentence: However, these __28__ less than 50% of the GDP now that Bhutan has become an exporter of hydroelectricity.
explanation: account for（佔……比例）為固定搭配，在此表示傳統產業如今僅佔國內生產毛額（GDP）不到一半；figure out（想出）、relate to（與……有關）、conform to（遵從）均不符合此處統計數據的語意。

sentence: Besides, cash crops and tourism are also __29__.
explanation: significant 意為「重要的、可觀的」：除了水力發電，經濟作物與觀光收入也相當可觀；durable（耐用的）、frequent（頻繁的）、expressive（有表現力的）都無法形容收入來源。
contextNote: also 掛在前一句 now that Bhutan has become an exporter of hydroelectricity 上——沒有前一句，「也很重要」就沒有比較的對象
note: 答對率 38%，鑑別度 49。【你可能會這樣想】四個形容詞看起來都能填，但要靠前一句才決定得了方向。【線索在前一句的 also】前一句說 these less than 50% of the GDP now that Bhutan has become an exporter of hydroelectricity（既然不丹已成為水力發電出口國，這些收入已占不到 GDP 的一半）——言下之意是水力發電已經是重要收入來源。本句以 Besides 起頭、句中又有 also，就是在「再加上一項同樣重要的」：經濟作物與觀光。沒有前一句，「也很…」就沒有比較的對象，所以這一格必須跨句判斷。「看到 also、besides、too，先回頭找它要對照的是什麼。」【干擾選項】durable（耐用的）用於物品；frequent（頻繁的）形容次數；expressive（有表現力的）形容表情或作品。三者都不用來形容收入來源。【常考搭配】be significant、a significant amount／impact、account for（占…比例）、an exporter of。

sentence: It was officially opened on June 11, 2005 as part of the celebrations to __11__ Norway's centenary as an independent country.
explanation: mark Norway's centenary（紀念挪威獨立百年）表示諾貝爾和平中心的開幕是紀念挪威獨立百週年慶祝活動的一部分；help／solve／take均不符合此處語意搭配。

sentence: ...it serves as a meeting place where exhibits, discussions, and reflections __13__ to war, peace, and conflict resolution are in focus.
explanation: related to war, peace, and conflict resolution（與戰爭、和平及衝突解決相關）表示展覽、討論與省思都圍繞這些主題；limited／addicted／contributed均不符合此處語意搭配。

sentence: The __16__ began 45 minutes before the start of the European Cup final.
explanation: tragedy（悲劇）呼應全文描述1985年布魯塞爾足球暴動造成多人喪生的慘劇；circumstance／sequence／phenomenon均不符合此處語意。

sentence: The British team was scheduled to __17__ the Italian team in the game.
explanation: compete against the Italian team（與義大利隊競賽）表示英國隊原定要與義大利隊比賽；oppose to（語意與文法不符，表示「反對」，且常用be opposed to）、fight over（為……爭奪）、battle for（為……而戰）均不符合此處運動賽事的語境。

sentence: The Italians, in panic, __19__ the main exit in their section when a six-foot concrete wall collapsed.
explanation: headed for the main exit（朝主要出口奔去）表示義大利球迷驚慌之下衝向出口，此時一道六英呎高的水泥牆倒塌；backed up（回堵、滯留／支援）、called out（大喊）、passed on（傳遞）均不符合此處衝向出口的語境。

sentence: Fish can "talk" to each other and make a range of __11__ by vibrating their swim bladder.
explanation: make noises（發出聲音）表示魚類透過振動魚鰾發出各種聲音來「交談」；choices／objects／accents均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Any goldfish lover who hopes to strike up a conversation with their pet goldfish is __15__.
explanation: out of luck（沒有希望的／注定失望的）表示想跟金魚聊天的人注定要失望，因為金魚聽力雖好但不會發出聲音；in the dark（不知情）、off the record（非正式的）、on the rise（上升中）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: __17__ forbidden to do so by the Congress, the agency for the first time will deliver mail only Monday through Friday.
explanation: Unless forbidden to do so by the Congress 是省略了主詞與 be 動詞的讓步子句（＝Unless it is forbidden…），意為「除非國會禁止」；When、Once、Lest 語意都不合。
note: 答對率 29%。【你可能會這樣想】句首 Unless 後面直接接過去分詞 forbidden，看起來像少了主詞和動詞，你就不敢選。完整寫法是 Unless it is forbidden to do so by the Congress——從屬子句的主詞與主要子句相同時，主詞連同 be 動詞可以一起省略。這正是國中學過的規則：While (he was) walking home, he saw an accident.。同類的還有 if necessary（若有必要）、when possible（可能的話）、unless otherwise stated（除非另有說明）。【怎麼判斷】主要子句說郵政署首度只在週一到週五送信；本句是這個決定的前提條件——「除非國會下令禁止」。前一句 This decision was announced on Wednesday without congressional approval.（這項決定未經國會批准）也在鋪陳同一件事：國會還沒表態。【干擾選項】When（當…時）與 Once（一旦）都表時間，語意不對；Lest 是「以免」，後面要接完整子句且用原形動詞。【常考搭配】unless（除非）、unless otherwise stated、if necessary、when possible、lest + S + (should) V。

sentence: It is expected that this __18__ will save about $2 billion a year.
explanation: this move（這項措施／這個做法）指前文提到取消週六送信的決定，預期此舉每年可省下約20億美元；round／chance／fact均不符合此處語意。

sentence: These germs come from the mouth and can __11__ in toothbrushes over time.
explanation: accumulate in toothbrushes（在牙刷上累積）表示這些細菌會隨著時間在牙刷上累積；crumble（崩解）、establish（建立）、radiate（發散）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Dinner is __16__!
explanation: Dinner is served!（晚餐上桌了！）為固定用語，此處幽默地表示黑猩猩挖出小昆蟲、準備享用大餐；proposed／ordered／digested均不符合此處語境。

sentence: The chimps make and use tools and have mother-child relationships very __19__ to those of humans.
explanation: similar to those of humans（與人類的相似）表示黑猩猩的親子關係與人類非常相似；casual（隨意的／非正式的）、direct（直接的）、grateful（感激的）均不符合similar to的固定搭配語意。

sentence: In the 1920s, he was sent to Europe as a newspaper correspondent to __12__ such events as the Greek Revolution.
explanation: cover 在此是「採訪報導」：一九二○年代他被派往歐洲擔任特派記者，報導希臘革命等事件；approve（批准）、predict（預測）、escape（逃離）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 35%，鑑別度 46。【你可能會這樣想】cover 你大概只記得「覆蓋」（cover the table）或「涵蓋」（The book covers three topics.）。在新聞語境裡它有一個固定用法：cover ＝ 採訪、報導某事件：She covered the war in Ukraine for the BBC.（她為 BBC 採訪烏克蘭戰事）。【線索是同句的身分】as a newspaper correspondent（擔任報社特派記者）——記者被派到歐洲，要做的當然是「採訪報導」。前一句 Hemingway started his career as a journalist at 17.（海明威十七歲就以記者身分出道）也在鋪陳同一條線。「空格前後若出現職業或身分，先想那個身分典型會做什麼動作。」【常考搭配】cover an event／a story（採訪報導）、a correspondent（特派記者）、coverage（報導）、cover the cost（支付費用）。

sentence: ...finally winning him the Pulitzer Prize he had long been __13__.
explanation: be denied sth 意為「被剝奪、始終未能獲得」：這本書終於為他贏得長久以來一直未能到手的普立茲獎；planned（計畫）、achieved（達成）、examined（檢視）都與 finally winning 的語意不合。
note: 答對率 19%。【你可能會這樣想】deny 你只記得「否認」（He denied stealing the money.），幾乎沒見過被動的 be denied sth ——「被剝奪、始終得不到」。例：I was denied the opportunity of learning French at school.（我在學校被剝奪了學法文的機會）／No one should be denied a good education.。【關鍵字是 finally】句中 finally winning him the Pulitzer Prize（終於為他贏得普立茲獎）——既然是「終於」拿到，代表先前一直沒拿到；過去完成式 had long been ___ 也標示這是得獎「之前」持續已久的狀態。【deny 兩義靠句型分辨】否認：deny + 名詞／V-ing／that 子句；不給予：deny sb sth。【常考搭配】be denied sth、deny doing sth、there is no denying that…、win sb a prize。

sentence: ...whose courage and honesty are set against the brutal ways of modern society, and who in this __14__ lose hope and faith.
explanation: in this confrontation（在這場對抗中）呼應前文set against the brutal ways of modern society（與現代社會的殘酷方式對抗），表示這些人物在與社會的對抗中逐漸失去希望與信念；limitation／classification／modification均不符合此處語意。

sentence: The three most common __16__ for road running events are 10K runs, half marathons (21.1K), and marathons (42.2K).
explanation: distances（距離）呼應後文10K runs, half marathons, marathons等具體距離單位，表示這是路跑賽事最常見的三種距離；journeys／destinations／measurements均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Road running is unique among athletic events because it __17__ all ages and abilities.
explanation: caters to all ages and abilities（適合／滿足各年齡層與能力的需求）表示路跑之所以獨特是因為它能兼顧各種年齡與能力的人參與；depends on／goes after／identifies with均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...even current world-class __18__.
explanation: world-class champions（世界級冠軍選手）呼應前文first time amateurs（初次參賽的業餘者）形成對比，表示路跑賽事讓業餘者與世界級冠軍同場競技；matches／civilians／associations均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Or, they may have __11__ you with the loud music from their earphones, or by taking up more than one seat on a crowded subway car.
explanation: offended you（冒犯你）呼應前文irritated（惹惱），表示他人可能用耳機外洩的音樂聲冒犯到你；controlled／acquired／supplied均不符合此處語意。

sentence: According to scientists, personal space involves certain invisible forces imposed on you through all the __12__.
explanation: through all the senses（透過所有感官）呼應後文unwelcome sound, smell, or stare（不受歡迎的聲音、氣味或注視），表示個人空間受侵犯的感受是透過各種感官傳達的；angles／events／regions均不符合此處語意。

sentence: Given the opportunity, they may __15__ to a corner, putting distance between themselves and strangers.
explanation: retreat to a corner（退到角落）表示有機會時，人們會退到角落以拉開自己與陌生人的距離；explore（探索）、dispense（分配／分發）、connect（連結）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: He was also the man who cracked the __16__ uncrackable Enigma code used by Nazi Germany.
explanation: supposedly 意為「據稱、一般認為」，帶有「後來證實並非如此」的暗示：他破解了那套「據稱無法破解」的恩尼格瑪密碼；eventually（最終）、precisely（精確地）、concernedly（關切地）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 33%，鑑別度 36。【你可能會這樣想】supposedly 這個副詞課本少見，而且它的作用很微妙——它不是在陳述事實，而是在標示「別人是這麼說的，但未必是真的」。【本句的趣味就在這裡】the supposedly uncrackable Enigma code ＝「那套據稱無法破解的密碼」。既然圖靈把它破解了，uncrackable（無法破解）顯然只是「大家以為」而已——supposedly 就是用來標記這個落差。少了它，句子會變成「他破解了無法破解的密碼」，自相矛盾。【怎麼判斷】看到形容詞前面要填副詞、而那個形容詞又與句子的事實衝突時，就要想到這類「據稱」的字：supposedly、allegedly（據稱，多用於指控）、reportedly（據報導）、seemingly（看似）。【常考搭配】supposedly + 形容詞、allegedly、reportedly、be supposed to（應該）、crack a code（破解密碼）。

sentence: This __20__ drove him to commit suicide in 1954, at the age of 41.
explanation: humiliation（屈辱）呼應前文Turing被判「嚴重猥褻罪」的羞辱，表示正是這樣的屈辱最終促使他在1954年自殺身亡；compromise／procession／supplement均不符合此處語意。

sentence: In 1902 Sir Archibald Garrod was the first to attribute a __20__ to an enzyme defect, which he later referred to as an "inborn error of metabolism."
explanation: attribute a disease to an enzyme defect 意為「把某種疾病歸因於酵素缺陷」：一九○二年 Garrod 首度將疾病歸因於酵素缺陷；balance（平衡）、measure（措施）、statement（陳述）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 39%。【你可能會這樣想】attribute A to B（把 A 歸因於 B）這個句型你若不熟，就抓不到 A 與 B 的關係：A 是結果、B 是原因。本句 B 是 an enzyme defect（酵素缺陷），那 A 自然是它造成的後果——疾病。【同句與下一句都在確認】句中 which he later referred to as an "inborn error of metabolism"（他後來稱之為「先天代謝異常」）——error、defect 都是負面的字；下一句舉了 PKU（苯酮尿症）與 galactosemia（半乳糖血症）兩種病名為例，直接把答案講出來了。「看到專有名詞或舉例，回頭對照空格，常常一眼就確認。」【attribute 的用法】attribute A to B ＝ 把 A 歸因於 B；被動是 A be attributed to B。同義說法有 owe A to B、ascribe A to B。【常考搭配】attribute A to B、be attributed to、refer to A as B（稱 A 為 B）、an enzyme defect。

sentence: ...it refers to the __11__ to one's neighbors as a standard for social status or the accumulation of material goods.
explanation: the comparison to one's neighbors 意為「與鄰居比較」，正是 keeping up with the Joneses（與人攀比）的核心；reaction（反應）、attachment（依附）、similarity（相似）都無法與 as a standard for social status 構成合理語意。
note: 答對率 33%，鑑別度 33。【你可能會這樣想】comparison 你認得，難在要先看懂 keeping up with the Joneses 這個文化片語。Joneses 是英文裡最常見的姓氏之一，用來代稱「隔壁鄰居」；整句意思是「跟鄰居比排場、不能輸給人家」，很接近中文的「輸人不輸陣」。【同句就有線索】it refers to the ___ to one's neighbors as a standard for social status（把…鄰居當作社會地位的衡量標準）——既然是「當作衡量標準」，那個動作必然是「比較」。而且空格後面接的是 to，comparison to／with sb 正是固定搭配。【干擾選項】similarity 是「相似之處」，語意接近卻不對——重點不是「像不像鄰居」，而是「跟鄰居比高下」；reaction 需要接 to 某個刺激；attachment to 是「對…的依戀」。【常考搭配】in comparison to／with、compare A with B、keep up with the Joneses、keep up appearances。

sentence: The __13__ was popularized when a comic strip of the same name was created by cartoonist Arthur R. "Pop" Momand.
explanation: the phrase（這個片語）呼應全文主題「Keeping up with the Joneses」這句慣用語，表示這個片語因同名連環漫畫而普及；signal／material／analysis均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...ran in American newspapers for 28 years before it was eventually __14__ into books, films, and musical comedies.
explanation: adapted into books, films, and musical comedies（被改編成書籍、電影和音樂喜劇）表示這部漫畫最終被改編成其他形式的作品；admitted／advanced／advised均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...they were merely __15__ but never actually seen in person in the comic strip.
explanation: spoken of（被談論）與後文but never actually seen in person（但從未真正露面）形成對比，表示瓊斯一家人只是被提及，卻從未在漫畫中實際現身；checked out（查核）、watched over（看顧）、traded with（與……交易）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: However, this generally __20__ when only a single or a few nails are affected.
explanation: apply 在此是「適用」：不過這種說法通常只適用於單一或少數指甲受影響的情況；indicates（顯示）、defines（定義）、confirms（證實）語意都不合。
note: 答對率 28%。【你可能會這樣想】apply 你大概只熟 apply for a job（申請工作）與 apply to（適用於，也用於申請）。它單獨使用時是「適用、成立」：This rule applies to everyone.（這條規則適用於所有人）／The same applies here.（這裡也一樣）。【線索要看前後兩句】前一句說白點會隨指甲長出去、除非再受傷否則不會回來；下一句用 If all of your nails are showing white spots… could be related to another more serious condition（若十指全是白點，可能與其他更嚴重的疾病有關）作對比。本句夾在中間、以 However 起頭，就是要限縮前面那個結論的適用範圍——所以是「這通常只在少數指甲受影響時才適用」。【常考搭配】apply to（適用於）、The same applies.、apply for a job、apply pressure、an application。
contextNote: this 指前一句「白點會長出去、不再出現」的結論；下一句 If all of your nails… 的對比才界定適用範圍

sentence: Folake's tasteful and colorful creations have earned her global __14__, making her the first African fashion designer to showcase her talent at the New York Mercedes Fashion Week for two consecutive years.
explanation: earned her global recognition（為她贏得全球認可）表示她品味出眾、色彩豐富的作品讓她獲得全球肯定；motivation／supervision／preparation均不符合此處語意。

sentence: She has also been widely __15__ in international media such as CNN.
explanation: be featured in 意為「被（媒體）報導、大幅刊登」，widely featured in international media 指經常在國際媒體曝光；believed、announced、populated 語意都不合。
note: 答對率 26%。【你可能會這樣想】feature 你大概只認得名詞「特色、特徵」，或是 feature 當動詞的「以…為特色」（The city features old buildings.）。用被動 be featured in 時，意思是「被報導、被刊登」，主詞是被報導的人或事：Her work has been featured in Vogue.（她的作品曾登上 Vogue）。【文內線索】前一句說她的設計為她贏得國際知名度、成為第一位登上國際舞台的非洲設計師；下一句說 2013 年她被《富比士》列為非洲最具影響力女性之一——前後都在講「媒體曝光與肯定」，中間這一格自然是「被報導」。【常考搭配】be featured in／on（被報導）、a feature article（專題報導）、feature（特色）、be listed as。

sentence: The submarine cables require extra __19__ to install.
explanation: require extra care（需要額外的小心／謹慎）表示海底電纜的安裝需要格外謹慎，呼應後文必須避開珊瑚礁、沉船等障礙物；speed／light／link均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...and stay clear of coral reefs, sunken ships, fish beds, and other general __20__.
explanation: obstacles（障礙物）呼應前文coral reefs, sunken ships, fish beds等具體例子，表示海底電纜鋪設須避開這些障礙物；directions／aquariums／circulations均不符合此處語意。

sentence: The Consumer Protection Commission, the current Consumer Protection Committee, __11__ the supervisor and coordinator for the various consumer protection organizations that put this law into practice.
explanation: serves as the supervisor and coordinator（擔任監督者與協調者的角色）表示消保會的職責是擔任各消保團體的監督與協調角色；fights for／persists in／corresponds to均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...the Committee advocates fair trade and __12__ pricing for goods and services.
explanation: reasonable pricing（合理定價）表示委員會提倡公平交易與商品服務的合理定價；prominent／essential／intensive均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...is active in assisting consumers involved in __13__ with businesses or manufacturers.
explanation: disputes with businesses or manufacturers（與企業或製造商的糾紛）表示委員會積極協助與廠商發生消費糾紛的消費者；references／shortages／purchases均不符合此處語意。

sentence: One morning in 1928, this __16__ proved very fortunate.
explanation: disorder 在此是「凌亂、無秩序」，回指前一句所說 Fleming 的實驗室一向雜亂：某天早晨，這份凌亂反倒成了幸運；merit（優點）、opinion（意見）、scandal（醜聞）都與前文接不上。
note: 答對率 30%。【你可能會這樣想】disorder 你大概只記得「疾病、失調」（eating disorder 飲食失調），沒想到它的字面就是 dis-（否定）＋ order（秩序）——也就是「凌亂、無秩序」。把字拆開，這個意思其實很直觀。【線索在前一句】Fleming had long been known for having an untidy laboratory.（弗萊明一向以實驗室雜亂出名）——untidy 就是 disorder 的同義改寫。本句用 this ___ 回頭指稱前一句講的那件事，這叫「概括性名詞回指」：用一個名詞把前面整句話收攏起來。遇到 this ＋ 名詞的空格，先回頭看前一句在講什麼，答案通常是那句話的濃縮。【怎麼確認】下一句說那天是他休長假後回來上班的第一天，接著就發現了盤尼西林——正是因為實驗室亂、培養皿沒收，黴菌才有機會長出來。【常考搭配】disorder（凌亂／失調）、in disorder、be known for、untidy、a stroke of luck（意外的好運）。
contextNote: this disorder 概括回指前一句 Fleming had long been known for having an untidy laboratory

sentence: While he was sorting through the long __18__ dishes of germs that morning, he observed that some of the dishes were contaminated with a fungus...
explanation: unattended dishes（無人照看的培養皿）呼應前文放假期間堆積在水槽裡的培養皿，表示這些長期無人照看的培養皿；disabled／excluded／misunderstood均不符合此處語意。

sentence: He was about to __19__ the dishes, but he noticed that in one dish, the bacteria had failed to grow in the area around the fungus.
explanation: toss out the dishes（把培養皿丟棄）表示弗萊明原本打算把這些受污染的培養皿丟掉，卻意外發現黴菌周圍沒有細菌生長；get off／catch up／carry on均不符合此處語境。

sentence: __11__ name phenomenon turns out to be very common.
explanation: Scrambled name phenomenon（名字混淆現象）呼應開頭Mothers often mix up their children's names（母親常把孩子的名字叫錯），表示這種名字混淆的現象相當普遍；Forgetful／Complicated／Alternative均不符合此處語意。

sentence: It is simply a result of the way our brain __12__ those we love.
explanation: classifies those we love（將所愛之人分類）呼應後文提到大腦以「群組」方式儲存所愛的人的名字，表示這是大腦分類記憶所愛之人的方式所致；defends／predicts／translates均不符合此處語意。

sentence: ...we are essentially __14__ the whole set of names in that group, which includes all our beloved family members.
explanation: flipping through the whole set of names（翻閱整組名字）表示大腦在尋找名字時，實際上是在瀏覽同一群組裡的所有名字；making up（捏造）、catching on（理解／流行）、skipping off（溜走）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: The only light that comes from TrES-2b is a faint red glow, __17__ the heated coils on an electric stove or a very dim light bulb.
explanation: similar to the heated coils on an electric stove（類似電爐上發熱的線圈）表示這顆行星發出的微弱紅光，類似電爐發熱線圈或昏暗燈泡的光；associated with／committed to／familiar with均不符合此處的比較語意。

sentence: Although they may never be able to __20__ exactly how the planet remains so stealthily hidden, scientists are sure of one thing---there are many more even darker planets out there just waiting to be discovered!
explanation: tell exactly how（確切查明／辨識出是如何）表示科學家或許永遠無法確切查明這顆行星如何隱身，但確信還有更多更暗的行星等待被發現；make／count／read均不符合tell exactly的固定搭配語意。

sentence: While __11__ stress can be dangerous to the body, short-term stress is actually healthy.
explanation: chronic stress（慢性壓力）與後文short-term stress（短期壓力）形成對比，表示長期慢性壓力對身體有害，但短期壓力其實有益健康；contagious（傳染性的）、diagnostic（診斷的）、tedious（乏味的）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: This __13__ us from physical discomfort and sickness.
explanation: shields us from（保護我們免受……）表示這個修復系統保護我們免於身體不適與生病；conceals（隱藏）、derives（衍生）、transforms（轉變）均不符合此處語意。

sentence: The skin with standing body hair __16__ a goose's skin after its feathers have been plucked---hence the name goose bumps.
explanation: resembles a goose's skin（類似鵝的皮膚）表示體毛豎立的皮膚看起來像拔毛後的鵝皮，因此得名雞皮疙瘩；imitates（模仿）、explains（解釋）、develops（發展）均不符合此處的外觀類比語意。

sentence: These days, however, goose bumps are __18__ to human beings.
explanation: no longer useful（不再有用）呼應前文提到雞皮疙瘩過去有助於保暖或嚇阻掠食者，如今對人類已不再具有實際功能；even more ridiculous／still quite convenient／more than important均不符合此處語意邏輯。

sentence: Many mammals fluff up their fur when __19__, to look bigger and thus more dangerous.
explanation: 【句型】when threatened為省略主詞的分詞構句，全句等於when they are threatened，主詞mammals與threaten之間是被動關係（哺乳動物「被」威脅），故用過去分詞threatened；threaten（原形）、threatening（現在分詞，主動）、to threaten（不定詞）皆不符合此處被動語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用)
sentence: One __11__ of using fossil fuels is that it can generate large amounts of energy by the simple process of burning.
explanation: advantage（優點）表示使用化石燃料的一項優點是能透過簡單的燃燒過程產生大量能源；grace（恩典）、source（來源）、problem（問題）均不符合此處正面語意（後文才轉折談到缺點）。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用)
sentence: ...fossil fuels are considered non-renewable resources, which will eventually decline in production and become __14__, with serious consequences to societies that remain highly dependent on them.
explanation: become exhausted（變得枯竭）表示化石燃料屬於不可再生資源，產量終將下降並枯竭殆盡；decayed（腐爛）、hollowed（挖空）、intensified（加劇）均不符合此處資源枯竭的語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用)
sentence: We are in general insensitive to architecture __16__ an art and to the joy it may bring to us.
explanation: insensitive to architecture as an art（對建築作為一門藝術感到遲鈍）表示我們普遍對「建築身為一門藝術」這件事不夠敏銳；or／of／like均不符合此處以身分／角色解讀architecture的語意搭配。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用)
sentence: It is the constant nearness of architecture during our life that has __17__ us in this way.
explanation: blinded us（使我們變得盲目／遲鈍）呼應全文主題——建築因與我們朝夕相處而讓我們對其美感變得視而不見；treated／guided／pleased均不符合此處「因熟悉而遲鈍」的語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用)
sentence: This false belief has strengthened in us until our eyes are __20__ and our minds are deadened to all the beauty that is being created around us today.
explanation: eyes are dulled（雙眼變得遲鈍）與後文minds are deadened（心智變得麻木）並列對應，表示我們對周遭美的感受力逐漸消失；pleased／justified／excited均不符合此處語意與並列結構。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: Cub Scouts, with the help of parents, build their own cars from wood, usually from kits __11__ a block of pine, plastic wheels, and metal axles.
explanation: 【句型】containing為現在分詞短語，修飾前面的kits（套件），表示「內含一塊松木、塑膠輪子與金屬車軸的套件」，kits與contain之間為主動關係（套件包含這些零件）；contain（原形動詞不能直接修飾名詞）、contained（過去分詞表被動，語意不合）、to contain（不定詞不符此處修飾語境）皆不符合。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: Murphy's son was too young to participate in the popular Soap Box Derby races, so he __12__ the idea of racing miniature wooden cars.
explanation: came up with the idea（想出這個點子）表示墨菲想出了讓孩子比賽木製迷你賽車的點子；dropped out（退出）、made out（辨認出／勉強應付）、put up with（忍受）均不符合此處語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: The Scout is given a block of wood made of pine __13__ two notches for wheels, four plastic wheels, and four nails.
explanation: a block of wood...with two notches（帶有兩個凹槽的木塊）表示童軍拿到的木塊上已鑿有兩個輪子用的凹槽，with在此表示「具有……特徵」；in／by／for均不符合此處的伴隨語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: Each __14__ is able to carve and decorate the car as he chooses.
explanation: Each scout（每位童軍）呼應全文主題Cub Scouts（幼童軍），表示每位童軍都能依自己喜好雕刻裝飾賽車；race（比賽）、master（團長）、parent（家長，但下文明確是男孩們自己動手）均不符合此處語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: The great thing about the race is that the boys build the cars with their dads, grandfathers, or __15__ they choose.
explanation: whomever they choose（無論他們選擇誰）表示男孩們可以和爸爸、爺爺或自己選擇的任何人一起打造賽車，whomever作為choose的受詞代名詞（指人）；whatever／whichever（指物）、wherever（指地方）均不符合此處指「人」的語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: Good manners make the social world easier to navigate. They provide guidelines to __16__ in times of uncertainty and reduce the chance of awkward exchanges.
explanation: guidelines to follow（可依循的準則）表示良好禮儀提供了在不確定情況下可依循的準則；confront（面對）、remove（移除）、lead（引導，但文法上lead the guidelines語意不通）均不符合此處語意搭配。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: A world __17__ good manners would be confusing and potentially confrontational.
explanation: a world without good manners（一個沒有良好禮儀的世界）表示假如世界缺乏良好禮儀，將會令人困惑甚至引發衝突；about／through／under均不符合此處語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: Good manners certainly connect people to their __18__ environment.
explanation: social environment（社會環境）表示良好禮儀能將人與其所處的社會環境連結起來，呼應後文a community and the people in it（群體與其中的人）；rural／clinical／mental均不符合此處語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: __19__ to adopt good manners sets people apart.
explanation: 【句型】Failing to adopt...為動名詞片語當主詞，fail to V表示「未能做到……」，指未能養成良好禮儀習慣的人會顯得與眾不同；Missing／Passing／Waiting均不符合此處fail to V的固定搭配語意。

source: 指考參考試卷(99課綱適用，102年施測)
sentence: We might forgive people who let their manners slip, but we'll feel more positive about and strongly connected to those who __20__.
explanation: those who don't（那些沒有這樣做的人）don't在此為助動詞構成的代動詞用法，代替前面省略的don't let their manners slip。全句與前文的who let their manners slip（禮儀失當的人）形成對比，表示相較於偶爾禮儀失當的人，我們對那些「始終保持禮貌、未曾失禮」的人感覺更正面、也更緊密相連；do／are／aren't均不符合此處的否定對比語意與動詞型態。
